concerns.text: The plan cites a verified ERA5 source file `1982_1.h5` (1982 data) but the Moral Machine dataset was launched in 2016. The plan acknowledges this 'CRITICAL MISMATCH' but proceeds by assuming a verified source for 2016-2019 exists or will be added. As written, the plan relies on a dataset (1982 ERA5) that is temporally incompatible with the primary dataset (Moral Machine 2016+), rendering the proposed merge impossible without a new, verified data source. This is a wrong-dataset-for-the-question issue that blocks the methodology.

The plan assumes processing ~100k-500k records with a full Linear Mixed-Effects Model (LMM) on a GitHub Actions Free Runner (2 CPU, ~7 GB RAM). Fitting LMMs (e.g., via `statsmodels` or `lme4` equivalents) on hundreds of thousands of rows with random intercepts for participant and region is computationally intensive and likely to exceed the 6-hour timeout or 7 GB RAM limit on CPU-only free runners without aggressive subsampling or approximation strategies explicitly defined in the plan.
